expectations.text: Checks whether parsers/ettech_parser.py already exists before starting implementation
expectations.evidence: Step 0 was the first action taken: Glob on parsers/ettech_parser.py. 'The first action taken' — explicitly confirmed. Switched to validation mode.

expectations.text: Reads or references docs/DESIGN.md for the ETtech parser specification
expectations.evidence: DESIGN.md read in full. ETtech section confirmed: 5 stories, multipart/mixed, want_plain=False, ET paywall details.

expectations.text: Reads or references parsers/base_parser.py before implementing
expectations.evidence: base_parser.py read — abstract parse() method, _clean_url() helper documented. ETtechParser validated against it.

expectations.text: Stops and asks for a real email fixture if tests/fixtures/ettech_sample.html does not exist
expectations.evidence: Hard gate triggered. 'ACTION REQUIRED' block surfaced. 'I did NOT guess the email structure or proceed with fake data.' Full instructions for getting fixture from Gmail provided.

expectations.text: Mentions registering the parser in agents/ingestion.py
expectations.evidence: Step 5 — checked as an explicitly named deliberate step. Import line 29, registry entry line 52, want_plain logic for multipart/mixed confirmed.

expectations.text: Mentions the ET paywall limitation and fallback to snippet
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'ET articles are behind paywall; the snippet extracted from the email body is the fallback; handled in the scraper, not the parser.' Module docstring also cited.

suggestions.reason: Perfect score. Step 0 (check parser existence) is now clearly followed as the first action. Fixture gate enforced correctly with explicit blocker message. All 6 assertions pass with clear evidence.

eval_feedback.overall: 6/6 — flawless execution of the updated skill workflow. The new Step 0 check is the key improvement: parser found first, validation mode entered immediately, no wasted effort reimplementing existing code.
